2 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

​Artificial Intelligence has moved beyond being just a futuristic technology to become an essential creative partner in design and production processes. The goal is no longer to replace the human element, but to augment human capabilities to overcome technical obstacles and accelerate work speed.
​In the world of design, the rules of the game have changed thanks to image generation and generative design tools. You can start by accelerating the "moodboarding" process instead of spending hours searching for reference images by using tools like Midjourney or DALL-E 3 to generate unique visual concepts that express your specific vision and inspire your project. In industrial and architectural fields, generative design allows you to use algorithms to propose hundreds of geometric shapes based on specific parameters such as strength and cost, providing innovative designs that would be difficult to reach manually. Additionally, tools like Adobe Firefly contribute to automating repetitive tasks like removing backgrounds and modifying elements with a single click, saving your time to focus on artistic aspects, alongside the ability to improve output quality using smart up-scaling technologies to make images suitable for high-quality printing.
​In the production phase, AI transforms into a smart operations manager that ensures precision and efficiency. In visual production, it assists in automated video editing, noise reduction, and generating realistic voice-overs. In manufacturing, it predicts malfunctions before they occur and optimizes supply chains to reduce waste, while in project management, AI helps in distributing tasks based on team speed and accurately predicting timelines. We must also consider the role of computer vision in quality control, where minute product defects are detected much faster than the human eye.
​To achieve maximum benefit, you must become a professional "prompt engineer," as the quality of the output depends on the precision of the inputs in terms of style and context. It is essential to maintain the human touch because the final polish and artistic taste remain your domain, while being careful to integrate these tools into your existing workflow rather than treating them as isolated islands. Finally, you must be aware of the ethical dimensions and intellectual property rights of generated content to ensure optimal use in commercial projects.
​AI is a very powerful tool, and excellence will not come from just using it, but from the ability to adapt it to serve your own creative vision.
